lifecycles._note: Closes the 1 remaining SG write gap: PUT /v1/security-groups/{security_group_id} (setsecuritygroup), an in-place update sub-op NOT covered by the existing 'networking-security-group' lifecycle (which only covers create SG + rule). Light, self-contained, REAL reversible create: SGs are account/region-scoped (no VPC needed, per validated VM/ske lifecycles, knowledge/validated-facts.md). Create SG -> capture $.security_group.id -> GET -> PUT (rename/redescribe) -> DELETE. PUT body (name/description/loggable) mirrors the proven createsecuritygroup body shape; the 'name' field reuses the proven SG name pattern. UNPROVEN: that setsecuritygroup accepts exactly these fields (no api_bodies.json entry for setsecuritygroup) — promote after a live 2xx. Distinct id from the existing 'networking-security-group' lifecycle (no collision).

steps.name: set-sg
steps._note: In-place update (setsecuritygroup). Body fields name/description/loggable mirror createsecuritygroup; UNPROVEN that PUT accepts these. optional+broad expect_status so a 4xx still records coverage without failing the run.
